title: Morning, World!
Aidan was the first to bulls-eye and pop his balloon on the archery range this morning!
Time for the Pirate activity at the ship in the woods.
Amazing how intense the boys can get about studying ecology!
Flag lowering is more casual tonight because of the two pig luau that's next. Clouds are a nice new feature. (I hope!)
Here is our pile of rain ponchos in the mess hall this morning. Kids didn't panic and even got excited to be camping in the Thunderstorm last night.
From the camp Childress' fishing pond with Aidan's newly gapped smile: Morning World!
Aidan cut the corner off the playing card target for tomahawk throwing. A memorable souvinir!
In remembrance of September 11th, Missouri Scouts retired over 2500 flags from across the state tonight, one for each victim, and more to honor those who have sacrificed themselves for our freedom.
This morning at 7:46 we observed silence, then remembered those who perished and those heroes who selflessly and namelessly came forward to help and support, to rebuild and stand united.

I just received an incredible shock: My entire neighborhood is gone. Demolished. Erased but for streets and poles. Another place from my childhood, a place from my high school years has joined much of my history and is irretrievably destroyed.
Aidan's Great Sales Pitch: Hi, I'm Aidan! It's popcorn time again! This is to support local scouts. Everyone loves our popcorn. What kinds would you like?
Father and Son. Living the good life!
Aidan just attended his first Missouri State Bears football game. Final score: Bears 7, University of N. Iowa 42.
Aidan at the park after another round of popcorn sales.

So long Caribe Royale!
It's been fun!
Spotted one of coolest slug bugs we've ever encountered this morning from Sea World. The dorsal fin and whale tail blend perfectly. Loved it.
Today was supposed to be a beach day. We took advantage of this window in the rain to get just a smidge of beach time in. Check out those storm clouds. Lightning started up again right after this.
Lunch at Felixes. An odd joint, to be sure...
We can see the battleship Alabama and the gulf of Mexico from our seats at Felix's. Good times.
Felix's: Looks like a giant pile of shacks, tastes pretty good, smells relatively bad, and priced like they are truly proud. Oh, well. I had the fresh grouper and I enjoyed it.

Meet A2-Z2. At Hollywood Studios, after the Star Tours ride (awesome!) is a place to build droids like R2-D2. Aidan built the droid he would like to accompany him on his adventures throughout the galaxy. Since Aidan's initials are ABCZ, I think he did a great job naming him A2-Z2, don't you?
Mom. Hurricane!
It's a lovely rainstorm. Need to bring it home!
